hawkish_phrases: no further rate cuts were imminent
interest rates were currently in a 'good place' and close to or at the end of the monetary policy cycle
Markets had fully priced out any rate cut at the current meeting
wage growth might decelerate more slowly than anticipated
there was a growing risk that average food price inflation might not revert to historical levels
dovish_phrases: a further rate cut in the coming months would better protect the inflation target
inflation was expected to decrease to 1.7% in 2026
risks to economic growth had become more balanced
trade tensions posed downside risks to inflation
the appreciation of the euro was still feeding through to goods inflation
summary: The Governing Council unanimously held all three key ECB interest rates unchanged in September 2025, with a neutral tone stating rates were in a 'good place' near the end of the cycle, though acknowledging a further cut would better protect the target amid balanced risks and an inflation outlook projecting undershooting in 2026.
